nodes.context: Family founded in early 1990s in Banská Bystrica, named after Mikuláš Černák, considered 'boss of all bosses' after 1997 underworld war.
Family founded in early 1990s in Bratislava, named after Ján Takáč, known as 'the group that outlived them all'.
Family founded in early 1990s in Bratislava, named after Miroslav Sýkora, oldest mafia group in Slovakia along with Takáčovci.
Family founded in early 1990s in eastern Slovakia, named after Róbert Holub, based in Košice and east.
Family founded in 1997 in Dunajská Streda, named after Tibor Pápay, involved in violent and economic crimes, nearly wiped out in 1999 massacre.
Family founded in late 1990s/early 2000s in Dunajská Streda, named after Ľudovít Sátor, enemies of Pápay gang, considered most brutal Slovak mafia group.
Family founded and named after brothers Ivan and Libor Jakšík, who worked with police and owned bars, had legal troubles in 2011.
Family founded in early 2000s in Lamač, Bratislava, named after Juraj Ondrejčák a.k.a. Piťo, known for using Mercedes G vehicles.
Family founded in early 2010s in Považská Bystrica District by twin brothers Dávid and Dominik Krátki, military-like drug group controlling several regions.
Powerful organized crime groups from Russia, limit Slovak mafia activities in Slovakia.
Organized crime groups from Ukraine, limit Slovak mafia activities in Slovakia.
Organized crime groups from Chechnya, limit Slovak mafia activities in Slovakia.
Listed as rival in infobox, involved in organized crime in Slovakia.
Various Balkan criminal groups controlling much of the heroin trade, limit Slovak mafia activities.

edges.context: Russian mafia limits activities of Slovak mafia in Slovakia.
Ukrainian mafia limits activities of Slovak mafia in Slovakia.
Chechen mafia limits activities of Slovak mafia in Slovakia.
Balkan groups control much of the heroin trade and limit Slovak mafia activities.
Albanian mafia listed as rival in infobox.
Pápay and his group began to cooperate with Miroslav Sýkor and his group in 1995.
Pápay cooperated with Mikuláš Černák and his group, attended vacation and birthday celebration together.
Sátorovci were enemies of the Pápay gang and eliminated them in 1999.
Černák helped Pápay take over his mentor Milan Sipos, and they cooperated.
